model_summary: This initial gVAR observation model targets six core financial-worry-related criteria (C01–C06) and five modifiable predictors (P01–P05) spanning PSYCHO, BIO, and SOCIAL domains. The predictor set emphasizes CBT-based worry management (P01), budgeting/financial navigation (P02), arousal regulation (P03), sleep circadian optimization (P04), and social-support engagement (P05). Data collection relies on brief momentary EMAs (4 prompts/day) plus a daily diary, with optional passive streams to contextualize intra-individual fluctuations, enabling a compact but estimable starting point for gVAR while preserving the complaint’s resolution.
variable_selection_notes: Variables selected to cover the complaint’s core constructs (intrusive financial worry, catastrophizing about expenses, chronic arousal, perceived basic-expense insecurity, low financial resilience, and future financial uncertainty) while prioritizing modifiable levers with clear, safe interventions. The five predictors were chosen to provide diverse, actionable levers across cognitive, behavioral, physiological, and social domains, minimizing redundancy and ensuring adequate within-person variance via frequent EMA sampling. Potential collinearity is mitigated by keeping the predictor set parsimonious and mapping each predictor to all criteria to enable a coherent early VAR structure.

momentary_ema.notes: 4 prompts/day distributed to cover morning, midday, afternoon, and evening windows.

daily_diary.notes: End-of-day reflection on finances, arousal, and sleep quality.

passive_streams.notes: Optional passive data streams (screen time, sleep proxies) to contextualize EMA signals.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Initial readiness high given manageable variable count and clear measurement protocols; ensure robust reminders to mitigate missingness.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: 4-point-to-9-point Likert-scale EMAs provide substantial within-person variance; all criteria and predictors use bounded ordinal or near-continuous scales to support gVAR estimation.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: Use automated prompts with reminders; supplement with a short daily diary to reduce missing data and provide anchor points for imputation.

predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Worry journaling; scheduled worry time; cognitive reframing; worry-to-planning exercises.

criteria.label: Chronic low-level physiological/affective arousal (panic)
criteria.query_text_used: Chronic low-level physiological/affective arousal (panic) | Operational item/signal: Since last prompt, rate your arousal/panic intensity on a 1-9 scale. | Polarity: higher_is_worse

mapping.rationale: The variable label 'Chronic low-level physiological/affective arousal (panic)' maps to a general, non-clinical arousal/panic construct in EMA contexts. The measurement is momentary self-report via EMA (4 prompts/day, smartphone), with the item 'Since last prompt, rate your arousal/panic intensity on a 1-9 scale' which aligns with the leaf path NON-CLINICAL / AGE-GENERAL / emotional / negative emotions / anxiety / panic. This is more specific than broad affective labels and avoids clinical panic-disorder leaves, matching the intended chronic, within-person arousal measurement. Among candidates, this node best matches the label, measurement type, and intended domain (arousal regulation via P03).

criteria.label: Perceived inability to reliably meet basic expenses
criteria.query_text_used: Perceived inability to reliably meet basic expenses | Operational item/signal: Since last prompt, how confident are you that you can meet your basic expenses today (rent/groceries) on a 1-9 scale? | Polarity: higher_is_worse

mapping.rationale: Best matching CRITERION leaf: idx 4741 labeled 'basic living expenses' (NON-CLINICAL / AGE-GENERAL / financial / financialwellbeing / abilitytomeetneeds / affordingnecessities / basic living expenses). The variable C04 is 'Perceived inability to reliably meet basic expenses' and the EMA item asks: 'Since last prompt, how confident are you that you can meet your basic expenses today (rent/groceries) on a 1-9 scale?'. This directly aligns with the leaf's focus on basic living expenses and financial sufficiency. The measurement mode (EMA_self_report) and momentary recall window ('since last prompt') are compatible with a leaf in a nominally non-clinical financialwellbeing domain that captures perceived ability to meet basic needs. Pseudoprofile context supports C04 as a core worry/constraint in financial domains without requiring clinical pathology.

criteria.query_text_used: Low confidence in financial resilience despite current adequacy | Operational item/signal: Since last prompt, rate your confidence in your ability to cope with financial stressors on a 1-9 scale. | Polarity: higher_is_worse

mapping.rationale: Direct alignment: var C05 measures a momentary self-rated confidence in coping with financial stressors (financial resilience). The best-matching, most specific leaf is idx 4723: NON-CLINICAL / AGE-GENERAL / financial / financialwellbeing / economicindependence / financialempowerment / *financial confidence building. It explicitly encodes confidence-building in financial contexts, matching the EMA self-report item (rate your confidence in your ability to cope with financial stressors on a 1-9 scale) and the mechanism of resilience under worry. Other candidates cover broader concepts (e.g., general self-efficacy, budgeting, literacy, or asset ownership) and are less specific to resilience confidence in the financial domain. The pseudoprofile context emphasizes modifiable levers for worry management; mapping to financial confidence building provides a precise operational construct for C05.

source_variable.label: Low confidence in financial resilience despite current adequacy

measurement.item_or_signal: Since last prompt, rate your confidence in your ability to cope with financial stressors on a 1-9 scale.

criteria.label: Future-oriented financial uncertainty
criteria.query_text_used: Future-oriented financial uncertainty | Operational item/signal: Since last prompt, rate your worry about the future financial situation on a 1-9 scale. | Polarity: higher_is_worse

mapping.rationale: Best match: candidate leaf idx 32054 'future_oriented_worry_rumination' (CLINICAL DSM-5TR ... cognitive rumination). It directly corresponds to future-oriented worry about a potential negative financial outcome, which fits 'Future-oriented financial uncertainty' and the EMA_self_report item 'rate your worry about the future financial situation'. The measurement mode (momentary 4 prompts/day via smartphone) aligns with the momentary assessment. The mechanism aligns with cognitive-emotional worry and rumination that CBT-based worry management targets; pseudoprofile context favors actionable CBT levers, but we do not overfit; this is the most specific operational construct mapping to the variable.

predictors.label: Worry management and planning (CBT-based)
predictors.query_text_used: Worry management and planning (CBT-based) | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Reduces perseverative worry via cognitive restructuring and worry scheduling; promotes forward planning and problem solving. | Intervention examples: Worry journaling; scheduled worry time; cognitive reframing; worry-to-planning exercises. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Since last prompt, on a 1-9 scale, how much have you been able to contain or reframe financial worries? | Prior ontology hint: '[PSYCHO] > Cognitive_Behavioral_Therapies > Perseverative_Cognition_Interventions'

mapping.rationale: Best-specific leaf for P01: It maps to Perseverative_Cognition_Interventions with worry_management/contain_and_reorient and worry_to_planning_discrimination, directly aligning with 'Worry management and planning (CBT-based)'. The measurement item (contain or reframe financial worries on a 1-9 scale) fits the containment/reorientation aspect, while the mechanism emphasizes cognitive restructuring and forward planning. This leaf is more specific to planning-focused worry management than other candidates (e.g., worry_container_practice, worry_postponement_skill), making it the strongest match.

source_variable.label: Worry management and planning (CBT-based)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Reduces perseverative worry via cognitive restructuring and worry scheduling; promotes forward planning and problem solving.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Worry journaling; scheduled worry time; cognitive reframing; worry-to-planning exercises.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since last prompt, on a 1-9 scale, how much have you been able to contain or reframe financial worries?

predictors.label: Budgeting support engagement (financial care navigation)
predictors.query_text_used: Budgeting support engagement (financial care navigation) | Domain: SOCIAL | Mechanism: Links financial resource navigation to reduced financial stress via access to funds and planning. | Intervention examples: Budgeting apps, automatic bill-pay, financial coaching. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Since last prompt, how confident are you in managing finances to cover upcoming essentials (rent, groceries) on a 1-9 scale? | Prior ontology hint: '[SOCIAL] > Socioeconomic_and_Material_Resource_Solutions > Budgeting_Support_Service'

mapping.rationale: Best-matching PREDICTOR leaf is Budgeting_Support_Service under SOCIAL > socioeconomic_and_material_resource_solutions > financial_and_benefits_support > financial_counseling_and_protection > budgeting_support_service. Variable label 'Budgeting support engagement (financial care navigation)' directly maps to budgeting_support_service. Measurement item 'Since last prompt, how confident are you in managing finances...' (EMA_self_report) aligns with budgeting/navigation support. Mechanism rationale (links financial resource navigation to reduced financial stress via access to funds and planning) supports budgeting as a targeted predictive lever in the gVAR model. Pseudoprofile context supports using this exact leaf to represent budgeting support engagement within SOCIOECONOMIC/resource domains.

source_variable.label: Budgeting support engagement (financial care navigation)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Links financial resource navigation to reduced financial stress via access to funds and planning.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Budgeting apps, automatic bill-pay, financial coaching.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since last prompt, how confident are you in managing finances to cover upcoming essentials (rent, groceries) on a 1-9 scale?

predictors.label: Arousal regulation (breathing/mindfulness)
predictors.query_text_used: Arousal regulation (breathing/mindfulness) | Domain: PSYCHO | Mechanism: Regulates physiological arousal to reduce panic-like sensations and cognitive reactivity. | Intervention examples: Paced breathing, brief mindfulness micro-practices. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Since last prompt, rate your current arousal or anxiety level on a 1-9 scale. | Prior ontology hint: '[PSYCHO] > Third_Wave_and_Contextual_Therapies / Mindfulness_Based_Interventions'

mapping.rationale: Selected leaf 'mindfulness_of_breath' (PSYCHO / third_wave_and_contextual_therapies / act_contextual_behavioral_therapy / present_moment_awareness / mindfulness_of_breath) as the most specific operational mapping for P03 Arousal regulation (breathing/mindfulness). The variable's label emphasizes breathing-based regulation and mindfulness, matches the measurement (EMA self-report of arousal on a 1-9 scale), and aligns with the mechanism of reducing physiological arousal and cognitive reactivity via brief breath-focused mindfulness. This fits within the Mindfulness_Based_Interventions subtree under PSYCHO as a direct arousal-regulation technique. Pseudoprofile context notes P03 as a modifiable lever with brief practices; 'mindfulness_of_breath' provides a concise, practical example.

source_variable.label: Arousal regulation (breathing/mindfulness)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Regulates physiological arousal to reduce panic-like sensations and cognitive reactivity.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since last prompt, rate your current arousal or anxiety level on a 1-9 scale.

predictors.label: Sleep circadian optimization (sleep routines)
predictors.query_text_used: Sleep circadian optimization (sleep routines) | Domain: BIO | Mechanism: Realigns circadian rhythm and sleep quality to reduce daytime arousal and cognitive load. | Intervention examples: Consistent bed/wake times; wind-down routine; limit caffeine late. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Yesterday night, how would you rate your sleep quality on a 1-9 scale? | Prior ontology hint: '[BIO] > Sleep_Circadian_and_Restoration > Sleep_Routines_and_Practices'

mapping.rationale: Match on variable: Sleep circadian optimization (sleep routines) measured with EMA self-report morning diary. The candidate leaf 4548 is 'sleep_quality_rating' under daily_and_retrospective_logs, capturing yesterday night's sleep quality on a 1-9 scale, aligning with the measurement item. It is the most specific operational construct among leaves that maps to a self-reported sleep quality rating in a daily log, consistent with circadian restoration and sleep routines domain. The mapping uses variable label + measurement item + mechanism rationale as primary evidence; pseudoprofile context supports P04 as a modifiable sleep routine lever.

source_variable.label: Sleep circadian optimization (sleep routines)
source_variable.mechanism_rationale: Realigns circadian rhythm and sleep quality to reduce daytime arousal and cognitive load.
source_variable.intervention_examples_safe: Consistent bed/wake times; wind-down routine; limit caffeine late.

measurement.item_or_signal: Yesterday night, how would you rate your sleep quality on a 1-9 scale?

predictors.label: Social connection/safety plan (resources & support)
predictors.query_text_used: Social connection/safety plan (resources & support) | Domain: SOCIAL | Mechanism: Enhances perceived support and access to resources, reducing financial anxiety via social buffers. | Intervention examples: Engage with social supports; join community programs; build a network. | Measurement: EMA_self_report | Since last prompt, rate your perceived social support and access to resources on a 1-9 scale. | Prior ontology hint: '[SOCIAL] > Social_Support_and_Belonging > Network_Building_And_Maintenance'

mapping.rationale: Variable P05 corresponds to Social_Support_and_Belonging with a focus on Network_Building_And_Maintenance and Resources/Context safety planning. The best-matching leaf is idx 1406: full path SOCIAL / social_support_and_belonging / network_building_and_maintenance / mapping_and_assessment / support_needs_by_context_assessment. This leaf directly captures network-building and context-specific assessment of support needs, aligning with a safety-plan style approach to resources and social buffers. The measurement item (rating perceived social support and access to resources since the last prompt on a 1-9 scale) fits a momentary assessment of social resources. Mechanistically, stronger social buffers reduce financial worry through buffering effects on stress and perceived insecurity, consistent with the predictor’s stated purpose. The pseudoprofile context supports this as a modifiable lever for within-person variance via EMA collection.
